title: Instance metadata endpoints
description:
## What
Implement or complete all instance metadata endpoints with correct shapes and real data from `config.toml`:
- `GET /api/v1/instance` — v1 shape (legacy, still called by all clients)
- `GET /api/v2/instance` — v2 shape (Mastodon 4.x, called by Elk and Ivory)
- `GET /api/v1/instance/peers` — return `[]` (no relay support in v1)
- `GET /api/v1/instance/activity` — weekly post counts, last 3 months
- `GET /api/v1/instance/rules` — return `[]` or configured rules from config
- `GET /api/v1/instance/extended_description` — return `{"updated_at": "...", "content": "..."}` (Ivory calls this on login; must not 404)

## Why
Clients call instance metadata on login to display instance info, configure character limits, and determine supported features. Ivory specifically checks `extended_description` and will show an error if it 404s. The v2 instance shape is required for Elk to display the compose character limit correctly.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: use curl against mastodon.social to capture the exact JSON shapes for all six endpoints; document every field in the v1 and v2 instance shapes that clients actually read (field names, types, default values for optional fields); note which fields must reflect real config (domain, title, description, character limit) vs. can be hardcoded (vapid_key = absent, registrations = false).
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: implement or fix all six endpoint handlers in `src/api/instance.rs`; populate fields from `config.toml` (domain, title, description, max_toot_chars, etc.); implement the activity endpoint with a real SQL query counting posts per week for the last 12 weeks.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests for each of the six endpoints asserting: correct HTTP status, all required fields present, field types match mastodon.social's shape; use the captured mastodon.social responses as oracle.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social /api/v1/instance and /api/v2/instance responses captured via curl; every required field name and type is derived from these captures.
- Test approach: integration tests asserting JSON schema compliance against the mastodon.social shape; field values tested against the test config.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- GET /api/v1/instance/extended_description must never return 404 — return a default empty content if not configured.
- All numeric fields (e.g., `max_toot_chars`) must be JSON numbers, not strings.
- `registrations.enabled` must be `false` (no signup flow in v1).
- `configuration.statuses.max_characters` in v2 must match `max_toot_chars` in v1 — they are the same limit, clients read from both.
- GET /api/v1/instance/activity SQL query must handle the case of zero posts (return weeks with count 0, not skip those weeks).
